phenomenon: Stroop effect modulation via proportion congruency
constructs: contingency learning prediction threshold facilitation interference
claim: Implicit learning of word-response contingencies, not strategic attention, explains proportion congruent effects on Stroop interference.

phenomenon: General activity regulation via goal priming
constructs: action inaction goal satisfaction outcome monitoring
claim: General action and inaction goals regulate motor and cognitive output across diverse behaviors independently of output type or valence.

constructs: moral agency moral patiency inverse relation dyadic schema
claim: Perceptions of moral agency and patiency are inversely related due to asymmetrical structure of moral dyads, independent of valence and causal direction.

phenomenon: Differential moral foundations in political ideology
constructs: moral foundations liberalism conservatism individualizing binding
claim: Liberals construct morality from two individualizing foundations; conservatives equally weight five foundations including binding concerns.

phenomenon: Disgust-driven moral intuition uncoupled from deliberate judgment
constructs: disgust sensitivity intuitive judgment moral condemnation implicit explicit
claim: Individual differences in disgust sensitivity predict intuitive moral condemnation of homosexuality independent of explicit judgment, revealing dual-process architecture.

phenomenon: Visual perspective bidirectionally links to action abstraction
constructs: visual perspective action identification abstraction third-person first-person
claim: Third-person visual perspective causally increases abstract action representation and vice versa, linking imagery viewpoint to conceptual level.
